what_it_does: Acceptance test that exercises the real scheduler and filesystem store to verify a failing node does not abort the whole run, is reported in result['failed'] with its error, writes no output, leaves downstream nodes stuck, and that a failure-free graph behaves unchanged.
observations: Acceptance-level test using real FsStore and scheduler.run, not mocks.
Constructs inline module-shaped node types with SimpleNamespace to include a deterministically raising node in the same graph.
Graph has two branches: an independent good branch and a failing boom->downstream branch.
Checks that scheduler.run completes without propagating the node's exception.
Inspects store output refs via store.head and store.get_content.
Includes a second clean run to assert failure-free behavior is unchanged.
Uses manual PASS/FAIL printing and returns 0/1 rather than a test framework.
standouts: Deliberately embeds a raising node to prove per-node failure isolation.
Asserts the run result surfaces failures in a failed map while independent nodes still run.
Verifies downstream-of-failed is classified stuck, not ran or failed.
conventions: Uses sys.path.insert relative to file location for local imports.
Defines inline fixtures as module-shaped SimpleNamespace objects matching scheduler's getattr expectations.
Executable acceptance script with __main__ guard and printed checks.
Imports contracts.shapes NodeInstance/Edge/Graph and store.fs_store FsStore.
concerns: Catches broad Exception to verify no propagation, which could mask unexpected infrastructure errors.
Inline node types bypass static type checking and may drift from real node module contracts.
No test framework harness or fixture cleanup on assertion failure.
Mutates the same global store directory between the failing and clean runs.
notes: Executable acceptance specification for scheduler failure isolation using real components and inline node-type simulations.
questions: What is the exact contract of scheduler.run's return value and stuck/failed/ran/skipped sets?
What does 'FIX 2' refer to in the docstring?
How does the scheduler classify a node as stuck versus skipped?
Are NodeInstance, Edge, and Graph validated at construction time?
Does the scheduler guarantee that a failed node's exception message is always stringified into result['failed']?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it demonstrates per-node error isolation and regression-safe clean-run behavior.
intention_signals: To harden the scheduler so an unhandled exception inside a single node is contained, reported, and does not abort or corrupt the rest of the run.
novelty: Simulates heterogeneous node modules (including a raising one) inside the test file using SimpleNamespace objects, turning an acceptance test into a self-contained executable script.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance contract for scheduler error isolation, plus deterministic graph fixtures and assertions that any fused scheduler implementation should satisfy.
summary_for_embedding: An executable acceptance test that runs the real scheduler against a graph containing a deliberately failing node to verify per-node error isolation, fail-loud reporting, stuck downstream handling, and unchanged clean-run behavior.
intention_for_embedding: To prove and preserve that a single node's runtime exception is contained and surfaced without aborting independent work or misclassifying unresolved downstream nodes.

symbols.does: Factory that returns a SimpleNamespace node type emitting a constant value.
symbols.description: Creates an inline module-shaped node type with VERSION, empty PORTS_IN, a single PORTS_OUT value port, and a run closure that ignores inputs and returns the captured constant.

symbols.does: Run function that always raises RuntimeError with a deliberate explosion message.
symbols.description: Used as the run implementation for the BOOM node type; it ignores inputs and config and raises an exception so the test can verify scheduler isolation behavior.

symbols.does: Constructs the iso graph with independent and failing branches.
symbols.description: Returns a Graph containing src_a->good and src_b->boom->downstream edges, designed so one branch succeeds while the other triggers a node failure and an unresolved downstream.

symbols.does: Reads the current CAS content for a node's output ref from the store.
symbols.description: Uses store.head on run://iso/{node_id} and, if present, store.get_content to return the node's produced output; returns None when no ref exists.

symbols.does: Prints a PASS/FAIL line for a labeled condition and returns the condition.
symbols.description: Simple assertion helper used by main to report each acceptance criterion and accumulate the overall pass/fail status.

symbols.does: Runs the acceptance scenarios and prints the final result.
symbols.description: Sets up the filesystem store, runs the isolation graph and verifies the six isolation properties, then runs a clean graph to confirm failure-free behavior is unchanged, returning 0 on success and 1 on failure.
